'''Corey Thompson''' (born 15 May 1990) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er and {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Brisbane Tigers]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].

He previously played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], [[Wests Tigers]] and the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League]], and the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Thompson was born in [[Brisbane|Brisbane, Queensland]], Australia, and is an [[Indigenous Australians|Indigenous Australian]].

He played his junior football for [[Mount Gravatt, Queensland|Easts Mount Gravatt]], before joining the [[Souths Logan Magpies]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2010 and 2011, Thompson played for the Souths Logan Magpies in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/news/rugby-league/story-e6frepo6-1225849750525|title=Story|publisher=Couriermail.com.au|access-date=2015-08-17}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.themorningbulletin.com.au/news/rugby-league-tyron-haynes-cq-comets/806469/|title=Haynes priming for big performance | Rockhampton Morning Bulletin|publisher=Themorningbulletin.com.au|date=2011-03-26|access-date=2015-08-17}}</ref> In 2012, he joined the [[Eastern Suburbs Tigers]],<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/questnews/southeast/walker-kauffman-big-winners-at-tigers-awards-night/story-fn8odwh7-1226488951409|title=Walker / Kauffman Big Winners at Tigers Awards|publisher=Couriermail.com.au|access-date=2015-08-17}}</ref> giving him the chance to do a pre-season with [[National Rugby League]] team [[Melbourne Storm]].<ref name="foxsports1">{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/league/nrl-premiership/former-baggage-handler-corey-thompson-is-set-to-make-his-nrl-debut-for-the-bulldogs/story-e6frf3rc-1226846326775|title=Former Baggage Handler Corey Thompson is Set to Make His NRL Debut|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-08-17}}</ref> The Storm attempted to sign Thompson but couldn't fit him under their salary cap.<ref name="foxsports1"/> In 2013, he played games for ICONZ Rugby in an Australian [[Rugby sevens]] tournament.<ref>{{cite web|author=Adrian Proszenko|url=https://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/nrl-bulldog-corey-thompson-desperate-to-fit-into-ben-barbas-old-jersey-20140208-328m2.html|title=NRL: Bulldog Corey Thompson desperate to fit into Ben Barba's old jersey|publisher=Smh.com.au|date=8 February 2014|access-date=2015-08-17}}</ref> In April 2013, while playing for the [[Eastern Suburbs Tigers]], he signed a 2-year contract with the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] starting effective immediately.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/questnews/southeast/easts-tigers-fullback-corey-thompson-has-earned-a-two-year-deal-with-the-bulldogs-in-the-nrl-effective-immediately/story-fn8odwh7-1226619151970|title=Tigers Fullback Corey Thompson Has Earned a Two Year Deal with the Bulldogs in the NRL Effective Immediately|publisher=Couriermail.com.au|access-date=2015-08-17}}</ref>

===2014===
In Round 1, Thompson made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for Canterbury-Bankstown against the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2014/round-1/canterbury-vs-brisbane/summary.html|title=NRL 2014 - Round 1|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=|access-date=2015-08-17}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=|url=https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2014/03/07/nrl_late_mail.html|title=NRL Late Mail|publisher=Bulldogs|date=7 March 2014|access-date=2015-08-17}}</ref> In September, Thompson re-signed with Canterbury on a 1-year contract.  Thompson made a total of 25 appearances for Canterbury in 2014 including the [[2014 NRL Grand Final]] which the club lost 30-6 against [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] at [[ANZ Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/south-sydney-rabbitohs-win-2014-nrl-grand-final-defeating-canterbury-bulldogs-306/news-story/8016f779194e71814b5118263ffbf713/|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs win 2014 NRL grand final, defeating Canterbury Bulldogs 30-6|website=Daily Telegraph}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/bulldogs-winger-thompson-signs-extension-14909/|title=Bulldogs winger Thompson signs extension | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2014-09-12|access-date=2015-08-17|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150821035230/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/bulldogs-winger-thompson-signs-extension-14909/|archive-date=21 August 2015|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=|url=https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2014/09/23/what_a_year_for_thompson.html|title=What a Year for Thompson|publisher=Bulldogs|date=23 September 2014|access-date=2015-08-17}}</ref>

===2015===
On 16 August, Thompson signed a 2-year contract with [[Super League]] team [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/news/article/44666/corey-thompson-signs-for-widnes|title=Corey Thompson signs for Widnes Vikings from NRL|publisher=Widnesvikings.co.uk|date=|access-date=2015-08-17}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/33953034|title=BBC Sport - Corey Thompson: Widnes Vikings to sign Canterbury Bulldogs back|work=BBC Sport |date=16 August 2015|access-date=2015-08-17}}</ref>

===2016===
In the 2016 season. Corey scored 27 tries for the [[Widnes Vikings|Vikings]], meaning he was the second highest try scorer in the league behind [[Denny Solomona]] of the [[Castleford Tigers]]. Corey helped his team to a 7th-place finish in Super League.{{cn|date=October 2022}}

===2018===
Thompson returned to the NRL with the Wests Tigers. With [[Tuimoala Lolohea]] pencilled in as fullback, Thompson was said to being adding "further depth" to the team.<ref>{{cite web| work= nrl.com| title=Tigers declare interest in Moylan| author=Alicia Newton| date=6 September 2017| url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/09/06/wests-tigers-declare-interest-in-matt-moylan/}}</ref> However, Thompson was named at fullback for the last trial before the season began, with coach Ivan Cleary saying post-match, "I couldn't have asked for more from him really. He was in everything tonight. Corey's been a standout right throughout the pre-season."<ref>{{cite web| work= nrl.com| title=Tiger Thompson trumps Lolohea for fullback spot| author=Dan Walsh| date=24 February 2018| url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/02/24/wests-tigers-corey-thompson-trumps-tui-lolohea-for-fullback-spot/}}</ref>

With Lolohea selected at fullback for the round 1, Thompson was chosen on the right wing ahead of regular winger [[David Nofoaluma]]. Near the end of the game, he scored a match-winning "freakish try finished with polish" as Wests Tigers defeated the Sydney Roosters 10-8.<ref>{{cite web| work= Fox Sports | title=Watch: Minimum wage winger Corey Thompson becomes Wests Tigers' hero in Roosters win| author=Nathan Ryan | date=10 March 2018| url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/tigers/watch-minimum-wage-winger-corey-thompson-becomes-wests-tigers-hero-in-roosters-win/news-story/42dc668dbbb53eb289a4e291af14ae12}}</ref> After Lolohea suffered a knee injury, Thompson was named at fullback for round 2.<ref>{{cite news| work= The Sydney Morning Herald| title=Nofoaluma back after injury strikes Wests Tigers' round one hero| author=Adrian Proszenko| url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nofoaluma-back-after-injury-strikes-wests-tigers-round-one-hero-20180316-p4z4ms.html}}</ref> Described as the, "early contender for bargain buy of the season". Thompson signed an upgraded contract for 2019.<ref>{{cite news| work= news.com.au | title=NRL's bargain buy of the year Corey Thompson rewarded with upgraded Wests Tigers contract | url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/wests-tigers/nrls-bargain-buy-of-the-year-corey-thompson-rewarded-with-upgraded-wests-tigers-contract/news-story/8b2668aecbe7e1cfb34d1ddc903f8ffe}}</ref> He continued to switch between fullback and winger, making 20 straight appearances before a season-ending ankle injury. Despite the premature end, he was the club's leading try-scorer with 9.<ref>{{cite web| work= Sporting News | title=Wests Tigers winger Corey Thompson reportedly out for season with ankle injury|author=Daniel Ramus | date=4 August 2018| url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/corey-thompson-ankle-injury-season-surgery-wests-tigers/a6cmukh0uzov1qzyyv92pmo04}}</ref>

===2019===
In Round 2 of the [[2019 NRL season]], Thompson scored 2 tries as Wests defeated the [[New Zealand Warriors]] 34-6 at [[Campbelltown Stadium]].  In Round 19 against Newcastle, Thompson scored 2 tries as Wests won the match 28-26 at the [[Newcastle International Sports Centre]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://7news.com.au/sport/rugby-league/tigers-pip-knights-in-10-try-nrl-thriller-c-368093/|title=Tigers pip Knights in 10-try NRL thriller|website=7 News|date=26 July 2019 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.tvnz.co.nz/one-news/sport/league/wests-tigers-punish-error-ridden-warriors-secure-commanding-win-in-sydney/|title=Wests Tigers punish error-ridden Warriors, secure commanding win in Sydney|website=TVNZ}}</ref>

Thompson finished the season making 22 appearances and scoring 9 tries as the club ended the year in 9th spot and missed out on the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/09/08/ruled-out-farah-gets-last-minute-call-up-after-warm-up-injury/|title=Ruled out, Farah gets last-minute call-up after warm-up injury|website=NRL|date=8 September 2019 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/robbie-farah-paul-gallen-private-chat-before-sharks-tigers/eebca170-6880-4a0b-8d1c-ddcc9088872a/|title=Robbie Farah's cryptic chat with Paul Gallen ahead of Leichhardt Oval showdown|website=WWOS|date=9 September 2019 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/nrl/story/_/id/27564311/cronulla-sharks-ruin-fairytale-wests-tigers-robbie-farah/|title=Cronulla Sharks ruin fairytale for Wests Tigers' Robbie Farah|website=ESPN|date=8 September 2019 }}</ref>

===2020===
On 15 June, Thompson signed a three-year deal with  the [[Gold Coast Titans]] after being granted a immediate release from the [[Wests Tigers]]. Thompson would go on to play 12 games for the Gold Coast in the [[2020 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.titans.com.au/news/2020/06/15/titans-tie-up-tiger-thompson/|title=Titans Tie Up Tiger Thompson|website=titans.com.au|date=15 June 2020 }}</ref>

===2021===
Thompson played 18 games for the Gold Coast in the [[2021 NRL season]] including the clubs elimination final loss to the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/09/11/hes-taken-it-hard-shattered-titans-rally-around-herbert-after-blowing-win/|title='He's taken it hard': Shattered Titans rally around Herbert after blowing win|website=www.nrl.com|date=11 September 2021}}</ref>

===2022===
Thompson made 13 appearances for the Gold Coast in the [[2022 NRL season]] scoring one try. The Gold Coast would finish the season 13th on the table and miss the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-gold-coast-titans-season-review-justin-holbrook-tino-faasuamaleaui-david-fifita-toby-sexton-jayden-campbell-kieran-foran-jamal-fogarty/news-story/271373bd289c9c920124d2847fde6d5e/|title=Titans' million-dollar man goes missing as Holbrook rues costly mistake: Brutal Review|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=8 September 2022 }}</ref>
On 10 November, Thompson signed a contract to join Queensland Cup side the [[Brisbane Tigers]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/former-widnes-vikings-and-super-league-winger-corey-thompson-finds-new-club/|title=Former Widnes Vikings and Super League winger Corey Thompson finds new club|website=www.totalrl.com|date=10 November 2022 }}</ref> and ultimately played fullback in the Tigers 22-18 victory over Burleigh in the 2023 grand final.<ref>{{Cite web | url=https://www.qrl.com.au/draw/qrl-premiership/2023/grand-final/game-1/ | title=Bears v. Tigers | date=2023-09-17 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231129230302/https://www.qrl.com.au/draw/qrl-premiership/2023/grand-final/game-1/ | archive-date=2023-11-29}}</ref>
